title: Lastrea japonica
taxonomy: Lastrea japonica is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the lady ferns or the wood ferns. It is native to East Asia, specifically in China, Japan, and Korea. The classification of Lastrea japonica can be broken down into its rank: it is a species within the genus Lastrea, which is further grouped into the subfamily Athyrioideae and the order Polypodiales. The family Athyriaceae is part of the larger class Polypodiopsida and division Pteridophyta. This complex classification highlights the fern's unique characteristics and relationships within the plant kingdom.
uses: Lastrea japonica is also known as Japanese lady fern. However, information on its medicinal uses is limited. Lastrea japonica has been traditionally used in East Asian medicine, particularly in China and Japan, for various purposes, including as a treatment for fever and as a diuretic. Its potential health benefits are thought to be due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. More research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety as a medicinal plant.
benefits: Lastrea japonica, also known as the Japanese lady fern, has been traditionally used in Eastern medicine to treat various health conditions. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress and promote relaxation. Some studies suggest that Lastrea japonica may also have antimicrobial properties, making it useful in wound healing and preventing infections. Additionally, the plant is rich in vitamins and minerals, including iron and calcium, which are essential for maintaining a healthy diet. However, more research is needed to fully understand the health benefits of Lastrea japonica and its potential uses.
properties: Lastrea japonica, a species of fern, is believed to have various therapeutic properties. It is often used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's extracts have been shown to exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Additionally, Lastrea japonica may possess analgesic and antipyretic properties, making it a potential treatment for pain and fever relief. Further research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of this plant.
constituents: Lastrea japonica contains various active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These compounds have been reported to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The flavonoids present in Lastrea japonica may also contribute to its potential cardiovascular benefits and ability to modulate the immune system. The phenolic acids in this plant have been shown to have antiproliferative effects, which could be beneficial in cancer prevention. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential therapeutic applications of Lastrea japonica's active constituents.

preparations: Lastrea japonica, also known as the Japanese lady fern, has been used in traditional herbal medicine for various purposes. The plant's fronds and roots are typically used to prepare infusions, teas, and decoctions.
